title: Executive Order 12250—Leadership and Coordination of Nondiscrimination Laws

flags.justification: The Attorney General receives broad coordinating and approval authority over all federal agencies' nondiscrimination enforcement, though bounded by an explicit carve-out excluding equal-employment coordination (Sec. 1-503) and by each agency retaining its own implementing authority subject to AG approval rather than AG takeover.
Sec. 1-304 requires an annual report to the President through OMB — a specific, concrete accountability mechanism.
Multiple nondiscrimination statutes (Title VI, Title IX, Section 504) are coordinated under one mechanism, all coherently addressing the same category of provisions.
Sec. 1-204's 'reasonable time limits' for initiating sanctions defers the concrete standard to the Attorney General's own subsequent guideline-development process rather than defining it here.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Permanence carve-out applies: nondiscrimination coordination is a standing, permanent government function, evergreen by nature.
Secs. 1-501 and 1-502 carefully preserve existing regulations issued under predecessor orders until formally revoked or modified — protective, continuity-preserving language.
Extensive interagency and state/local coordination requirements (memoranda of understanding, information sharing, cooperative programs per Sec. 1-206) provide real structure.

Establishes a major, still-operative institutional structure for coordinating federal civil-rights enforcement across dozens of agencies — the resulting DOJ coordination regulations (28 CFR Part 42) remain the operative framework decades later.

Each agency retains its own implementing authority subject to AG coordination and approval rather than direct takeover, and Sec. 1-503 explicitly excludes equal-employment coordination from this authority.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 3/20 = 15.0%. A significant, still-operative institutional structure for federal civil-rights enforcement coordination.
